cases.description: [boolean-flip] src/core/interrupt-reminder.ts:36 â€” A boolean literal on line 36 was flipped to its opposite value.
cases.responsePreview: Fixed: `return false` â†’ `return true` on line 36. The function was returning the opposite of what it should â€” when it finds `[ABORTED]` in a trailing tool message, it needs to signal `true` so `appendInterruptReminder` knows to skip the reminder.

trace.thinking: Let me read the file first.

The bug is on line 36. The function `trailingToolsSignalUserAbort` is supposed to return `true` when trailing tool messages signal a user abort (i.e., they contain "[ABORTED]"). But on line 36, it returns `false` when it finds "[ABORTED]" â€” it should return `true`.

Wait, let me re-read the docstring. It says: "Skip the reminder â€” the tool results are enough." So if trailing tools signal a user abort, we should skip the reminder. And `appendInterruptReminder` returns early when `trailingToolsSignalUserAbort` returns true.

So `trailingToolsSignalUserAbort` should return `true` when it finds "[ABORTED]" in a tool message. But line 36 returns `false`. That's the bug â€” `false` should be `true`.
trace.commentary: The function `trailingToolsSignalUserAbort` should return `true` when it finds `[ABORTED]` in a trailing tool message, but line 36 returns `false`.

Fixed: `return false` â†’ `return true` on line 36. The function was returning the opposite of what it should â€” when it finds `[ABORTED]` in a trailing tool message, it needs to signal `true` so `appendInterruptReminder` knows to skip the reminder.
